LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT CORPORATION'S MOTION TO REINSTATE DEFAULT IS DENIED. WHATEVER REMEDIES MAY BE AVAILABLE FOR I-TEC ELECTRONICS' FAILURE TO PAY FEES PER THE JANUARY 18, 2013 ORDER, THEY DO NOT INCLUDE VACATING THE RELIEF FROM DEFAULT. THE COURT SIGNED THE ORDER THAT WAS EMAILED TO THE COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT I-TEC ELECTRONICS, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE TO GRANT THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT. PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473(B), DEFENDANT SHALL PAY $5000 TO PLAINTIFF FOR ATTORNEYS FEES WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-18-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO REINSTATE DEFUALT;, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT CORPORATION HEARING SET FOR MAY-06-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW'S MOTION TO IMPOSE SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 177.5. DEFENDANT VIOLATED THE COURT'S JANUARY 18, 2013 ORDER TO PAY PLAINTIFF $5000 IN ATTORNEY'S FEES WITHIN 30 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE OR SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. THE AFFIDAVIT OF DAVID KRAUS, ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT C TO THE PAPERS IN OPPOSITION, IS UNDATED. PLAINTIFF CONTENDS THAT THIS DECLARATION IS A YEAR OLD. WITHOUT MORE, THE COURT CANNOT FIND ON THE RECORD NOW BEFORE THE COURT THAT THERE IS CURRENTLY GOOD CAUSE OR SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. DEFENDANT IS SANCTIONED $100 TO BE PAID TO THE COURT WITHIN 30 DAYS. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AND EXPENSES PURSUANT TO CRC 2.30(B) AND (D) IS DENIED. ACCORDING TO PLAINTIFF, THERE IS A PENDING ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING SET IN DEPARTMENT 610 WHERE THE MATTER SHOULD BE ADDRESSED. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF CAN SEEK JUDGMENT TO ENFORCE A MONEY ORDER UNDER CCP 680.230 AND 680.270. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

DISCOVERY 302, LAINTIFF AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTEROGGATORIES IS GRANTED. SANCTIONS OF $3,000, PAYABLE WITHIN 10 DAYS TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, ARE AWARDED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES. P. WATSON, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jul-05-2013 Continued To Aug-14-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Mtn To Impose Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT PERL PERLMUTTER OF SHUTE MIHALY & WEINBERGER LLP, TEL. NO. 552-7272 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GARY M. ANDERSON OF FUWLWIDER PATTON LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 824-5555 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW's motion To Impose Sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is granted pursuant to CCP 177.5. Defendant violated the Court's January 18, 2013 order to pay Plaintiff $5000 in attorney's fees within 30 days of entry of the order without good cause or substantial justification. The affidavit of David Kraus, attached as Exhibit C to the papers in opposition, is undated. Plaintiff contends that this declaration is a year old. Without more, the Court cannot find on the record now before the Court that there is currently good cause or substantial justification. Defendant is sanctioned $100 to be paid to the Court within 30 days. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions and expenses pursuant to CRC 2.30(b) and (d) is denied. According to Plaintiff, there is a pending order to show cause hearing set in Department 610 where the matter should be addressed. Finally, Plaintiff can seek judgment to enforce a money order under CCP 680.230 and 680.270. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reinstate Defualt;
Off Calendar May-06-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gary M. Anderson, Esq. of Fulwider Patton LLP, Tel. 310.824.5555, for defendant I-tec Electronics by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested. PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT CORPORATION'S Motion To Reinstate Default is denied. Whatever remedies may be available for I-Tec Electronics' failure to pay fees per the January 18, 2013 order, they do not include vacating the relief from default. The Court signed the order that was emailed to the court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jan-18-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gary M. Anderson, Esq. of Fulwider Patton LLP, Tel. 310.824.5555, for defendant and moving party;
Robert Perlmutter, Esq. of Shute Mihaly & Weinberger LLP, Tel. 415.552.7272, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT I-TEC ELECTRONICS, INC.'s MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is Granted. Defendant has shown good cause to grant the motion to set aside the default. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 473(b), Defendant shall pay $5000 to Plaintiff for attorneys fees within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. Order signed. Judge: Marla J.
